bio: Majid Sharif was a writer, translator, and sociologist — a great-grandson of Ayatollah Habib Sharif Kashani. He held a Ph.D. in sociology. On 19 November 1998, Sharif disappeared during a morning jog. His body was found on 25 November; the official cause of death was listed as 'cardiac arrest,' but it was later established that he had been killed via potassium injection by agents of the [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence). His death, occurring just days before the murder of Dariush and Parvaneh Forouhar, was part of the [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) campaign.
editorial_notes: Born 1950. Writer and sociologist. PhD. Disappeared 19 November 1998, body found 25 November. Killed by potassium injection. Great-grandson of Ayatollah Habib Sharif Kashani.
